data.title: As a practitioner, I can add and remove tags on any ticket via first-class CLI commands
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add `ticket tag <id> <tag>` and `ticket untag <id> <tag>` CLI commands as first-class tag-manipulation operations, backed by shared helper functions in `ticket-lib.sh` and thin wrapper scripts.

**Why**: Eliminates duplicated read-merge-write logic in downstream call sites (handled in Story 3), establishes tag manipulation as a first-class ticket operation, and sets up the helper structure that Story 2's PIL guard wraps.

**Scope**:
- IN:
  - `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-tag.sh` and `plugins/dso/scripts/ticket-untag.sh` thin wrapper scripts that source `ticket-lib.sh`
  - New helpers in `ticket-lib.sh`: `_tag_add` (bare add; no PIL guard — Story 2 introduces `_tag_add_checked` as a wrapper) and `_tag_remove`
  - `.claude/scripts/dso` shim entries for `tag` and `untag` subcommands
  - Idempotent behavior: re-running exits 0 with no new event when the tag is already present (add) or already absent (remove)
  - Sibling-tag preservation on both add and remove
  - Empty-tag-set handling: when `untag` removes the last tag, emit an EDIT event with `data.fields.tags: []` (matching ticket-edit.sh's existing shape, NOT `data.tags: []`)
- OUT:
  - PIL guard for `brainstorm:complete` — Story 2
  - Refactor of existing call sites — Story 3

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, running `.claude/scripts/dso ticket tag <id> <tag>` on a ticket without that tag appends it, preserving all other tags, and re-running exits 0 with no new event.
  ← Satisfies: "`ticket tag <id> <tag>` adds a single tag to a ticket tag set and preserves all existing tags; command succeeds idempotently when the tag is already present."
- When this story is complete, running `.claude/scripts/dso ticket untag <id> <tag>` on a ticket with that tag removes only that tag, preserving all other tags, and re-running exits 0 idempotently.
  ← Satisfies: "`ticket untag <id> <tag>` removes a single tag and preserves all other tags; command succeeds idempotently when the tag is already absent."
- When this story is complete, `ticket untag` that removes the last remaining tag emits an EDIT event with `data.fields.tags: []` (not `data.tags: []`), verified by a unit test asserting the serialized event JSON shape.
  ← Satisfies: "`ticket untag <id> <tag>` removes a single tag and preserves all other tags; command succeeds idempotently when the tag is already absent."
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic.

## Considerations

- [Testing] Bash tests required: add on empty/non-empty ticket, remove on single/multi-tag ticket, idempotent re-runs, sibling preservation, empty-tag-set serialization shape
- [Reliability] Read-merge-write race between concurrent agents accepted — same tolerance as existing `ticket edit --tags=`; documented in the helper's header comment
- [Maintainability] Helper lib centralizes logic later reused by Story 3's 6 call-site refactor

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
